title: Design modules with explicit input/output contracts
description: Compose infrastructure from modules that expose typed variables and outputs, avoiding hidden coupling and copy-paste resource blocks.
body: Use when the same resource pattern repeats or a stack grows beyond a few files. Do not copy-paste resource blocks across environments, and do not reach into a module's internal resources from outside. Actions: extract reusable patterns into a module with typed input variables (with validation), expose only needed outputs, and pin module sources to a version. Validation signals include the module instantiated in multiple environments with only inputs differing and terraform validate passing. Risk boundary: an over-broad module that takes a provider or raw resource id as input can let a caller mutate unintended infrastructure; keep module surface minimal.
